sanskrit: पुरुषः प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङ्क्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान् । कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु ॥
hindiTranslation: प्रकृतिमें स्थित पुरुष ही प्रकृतिजन्य गुणोंका भोक्ता बनता है और गुणोंका सङ्ग ही उसके ऊँचनीच योनियोंमें जन्म लेनेका कारण बनता है ॥
englishTranslation: The inner being, situated in nature, experiences the qualities born of nature. Attachment to those qualities causes birth in good and bad wombs.
essence: Attachment to changing qualities keeps the cycle of birth going.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The inner being, while tied to nature, experiences its changing qualities. Clinging to those qualities is what leads to repeated births in favorable and unfavorable forms.
simpleInsight: What traps you is not experience itself, but your attachment to its changing moods and pulls.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen, and Krishna is explaining why suffering repeats. The inner being, caught in nature, tastes its changing qualities; that same attachment becomes the cause of future births in better or worse conditions.
modernRelevance: You keep replaying the same argument in your head after the call ends. The event is over, but your attachment to its emotional charge keeps pulling you back.
translationLiteral: पुरुषः (the inner being) / प्रकृतिस्थः (situated in nature) / हि (indeed) / भुङ्क्ते (experiences) / प्रकृतिजान् (born of nature) / गुणान् (qualities) / कारणम् (cause) / गुणसङ्गः (attachment to qualities) / अस्य (of this) / सत्-असत् (good and bad) / योनि-जन्मसु (in womb-births)
